---
title: "Ingest Xenium / Visium"
description: "Space Ranger mapping and spatial ingestion."
categories: [openpipeline_spatial]
order: 15
---

[`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial}<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>

Spatial ingestion lives in the **`openpipeline_spatial`** package, which pins the
core `openpipeline` as a Viash dependency and adds Space Ranger mapping plus
spatial-aware processing.

## Map with Space Ranger

`ingestion/spaceranger_mapping` runs 10x Space Ranger to align a Visium or
Xenium run and produce a spatial `.h5mu` — the count matrix carries per-spot (or
per-cell) coordinates in `.obsm["spatial"]`.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/spaceranger_mapping/main.nf \
  --id sample_A \
  --input "fastq/sample_A/" \
  --reference reference_genome/ \
  --output sample_A.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Process spatial samples

After ingestion, `multiomics/spatial_process_samples` runs QC, normalization,
and dimensionality reduction adapted for spatial data, and `qc/spatial_qc`
produces spatial quality-control output. These mirror the core
[process & filter](process-filter.qmd) flow but keep the spatial coordinates
intact.

:::{.callout-note}
`openpipeline_spatial` is a separate package. Check the
[reference](../reference/index.qmd) (filter by `openpipeline_spatial`) for the
exact argument list of each spatial workflow, and open its Viash Hub page for
copy-paste run instructions.
:::

**Next:** [Find spatial niches →](spatial-niches.qmd) ·
[spatial reference →](../reference/index.qmd)
